data.title: Buy Rating Reaffirmed on Solid Fundamentals and Credit Trends; $29 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
In a report released today, Caroline Latta from Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating on SLM, with a price target of $29.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Caroline Latta has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that, in her view, outweigh the headline EPS shortfall in the recent quarter. She highlights that the earnings miss was driven primarily by a temporary spike in provisions linked to changes in recovery practices, while management reaffirmed full-year EPS, loan origination, and expense guidance, signaling confidence in underlying performance.

Latta also points to SLM’s solid competitive positioning, long-term upside from the Grad PLUS opportunity, and a supportive funding structure that underpins capital return to shareholders. She expects net interest margin to improve in the second half as excess liquidity is deployed, and notes that credit trends, including delinquencies and loan-mod performance, are slightly better than previously anticipated, supporting her unchanged $29 price target.

In another report released today, Wells Fargo also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $28.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Prime Medicine Buy Rating Reiterated as Pipeline Advances; $6 Price Target Maintained
data.description:
LifeSci Capital analyst Cory Jubinville, PhD maintained a Buy rating on Prime Medicine, Inc. today and set a price target of $6.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Cory Jubinville, PhD has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Prime Medicine’s emerging clinical pipeline and regulatory momentum. The recent FDA clearance of the IND for PM577a in Wilson disease, alongside the earlier New Zealand CTA, establishes a global Phase 1/2 program that is expected to begin in the second half of 2026 and generate initial clinical readouts in 2027, providing a clear path to value-inflecting milestones.

Jubinville also highlights that PM577a is designed to correct the most common ATP7B mutation driving Wilson disease in North America and Europe, giving the program substantial addressable-market relevance while follow-on candidates, such as an R778L-focused therapy for East Asian patients, expand the broader opportunity. Despite a relatively short cash runway and notable short interest, he maintains that the company’s focused execution, multi-asset rare disease strategy, and defined upcoming catalysts support a favorable risk/reward profile and justify reiterating a Buy rating with an unchanged $6 price target.

In another report released on July 9, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $8.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Buy Rating Reiterated on Integra Resources as Strong 2Q26 Performance and Florida Canyon Feasibility Support Unchanged $6.75 Price Target
data.description:
In a report released today, Heiko Ihle from H.C. Wainwright reiterated a Buy rating on Integra Resources Corp, with a price target of $6.75.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Heiko Ihle has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Integra Resources’ operational momentum and outlook. The company delivered a strong 2Q26 with materially higher gold output, driven by increased ore mining, greater tonnage placed on the heap leach pads, improved grades, and a lower strip ratio, while management reaffirmed its 2026 guidance of 70,000–75,000 ounces, suggesting confidence in stronger second-half performance.

At the same time, the Florida Canyon Feasibility Study underpins longer-term value by outlining a longer mine life, significantly higher reserves, and substantial projected after-tax free cash flow that can help fund DeLamar’s development. Ihle’s valuation, based on a discounted cash flow model using differentiated discount rates for DeLamar, Florida Mountain, and Florida Canyon, plus additional value for Wildcat, Mountain View, exploration assets, and cash, supports a net asset value that translates into a rounded price target of $6.75, which remains unchanged and underlies his Buy recommendation.

Ihle covers the Basic Materials sector, focusing on stocks such as Gold Royalty, 5E Advanced Materials, Inc., and Avino Silver & Gold. According to TipRanks, Ihle has an average return of 40.9% and a 52.06% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Balanced Outlook for Graco: Improving Orders and Backlog Offset by Modest Near-Term Growth, Supporting Hold Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Ross Sparenblek has maintained their neutral stance on GGG stock, giving a Hold rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ross Sparenblek has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including Graco’s modest current growth profile and improving order trends. While second-quarter organic revenue slipped slightly, orders and backlog expanded meaningfully, supported by recovering demand in key end markets such as semiconductors, AI-related data centers, and broader industrial activity. This improving order momentum underpins management’s outlook for a gradual acceleration in the second half.

At the same time, the near-term growth outlook remains relatively low, with management guiding to only low-single-digit organic revenue expansion and third-quarter sales that closely track current expectations. The recent earnings beat also benefited from nonrecurring items, tempering the quality of the upside. Taken together—solid backlog, healthier bookings, but only modest forecast growth and some reliance on one-time benefits—these factors support a balanced risk-reward profile that aligns best with a Hold rating at this stage.

According to TipRanks, Sparenblek is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 21.0% and a 75.31% success rate. Sparenblek covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Federal Signal, JBT Marel, and MSA Safety.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reiterates Buy on BridgeBio, Maintaining $120 Price Target as Late-Stage Rare Disease Pipeline Advances
data.description:
H.C. Wainwright analyst Ram Selvaraju has reiterated their bullish stance on BBIO stock, giving a Buy rating on July 22.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Ram Selvaraju has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including the advancement of encaleret toward potential approval and the breadth of BridgeBio’s late-stage pipeline. The FDA’s acceptance of the New Drug Application for encaleret in autosomal dominant hypocalcemia type 1, supported by a successful Phase 3 trial and strong efficacy data, underpins confidence in a meaningful new revenue stream targeting a growing diagnosed patient population.

He also highlights that BridgeBio could have four differentiated therapies on the U.S. market by next year, spanning acoramidis, ribitol, encaleret and infigratinib, each focused on high-value rare disease segments. Favorable regulatory designations for infigratinib, ongoing global trials, and the planned U.S. and EU filings collectively support sustained growth prospects, leading him to reiterate a Buy rating and maintain a $120 price target.

In another report released on July 22, Bank of America Securities also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $101.00 price target.
